annotations.label: Hölder's inequality

anchor.value: Hölder's inequality — Let [MATH] be a me

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the central Hölder inequality `∫ f·g ≤ (∫ fᵖ)^(1/p)·(∫ gᵍ)^(1/q)` for conjugate exponents and ℝ≥0∞-valued measurable functions.

annotations.label: Conventions for Hölder conjugates

anchor.snippet: In the definition of Hölder conjugates, 1/∞ means zero.

annotations.note: `ENNReal.HolderConjugate p q` is defined as `p⁻¹ + q⁻¹ = 1` in `ℝ≥0∞` where `(∞)⁻¹ = 0`, exactly matching the Wikipedia convention.

annotations.label: Essential supremum convention

anchor.snippet: stands for the essential supremum of

annotations.note: `essSup f μ` is defined as `(ae μ).limsup f`, capturing the essential supremum of a measurable function.

annotations.label: Cauchy–Schwarz inequality (L² case)

anchor.snippet: if f and g are in the Hilbert space

annotations.note: The L² Cauchy–Schwarz inequality is the special case p=q=2 of Mathlib's Hölder inequality for Bochner integrals; the abstract Hilbert-space version is `norm_inner_le_norm`.

annotations.label: Hölder's inequality on a probability space

anchor.snippet: just need to satisfy 1/ p + 1/ q ≤ 1 , rather than being Hölder conjugates

annotations.note: Mathlib has the underlying pieces (Hölder + `MemLp.mono_exponent` for finite measures) but no single decl stating the relaxed `1/p + 1/q ≤ 1` Hölder on a probability space.

annotations.label: Hölder's inequality for counting measure

anchor.snippet: with the counting measure

annotations.note: Finite-sum Hölder inequalities for NNReal- and Real-valued sequences (`inner_le_Lp_mul_Lq` and variants) formalize the counting-measure case directly.

annotations.label: Generalization to more than two sums

anchor.snippet: For more than two sums, the following generalisation

annotations.note: The multi-function Hölder `lintegral_prod_norm_pow_le` covers the counting-measure (sums) case as a special instance.

annotations.label: Hölder's inequality for sequence spaces

anchor.snippet: then we get Hölder's inequality for sequence spaces

annotations.note: `lp.tsum_mul_le_mul_norm` (and `NNReal.inner_le_Lp_mul_Lq_tsum`) state Hölder for the sequence spaces `ℓᵖ` and `ℓ^q`.

annotations.label: Hölder's inequality for Lebesgue measure

anchor.snippet: is a measurable subset of

annotations.note: The Lebesgue-measure case follows by specializing the general Bochner Hölder inequality `integral_mul_norm_le_Lp_mul_Lq` to `volume`.

annotations.label: Hölder's inequality for random variables

anchor.snippet: Hölder's inequality reads

annotations.note: The probability/random-variable formulation `E[|XY|] ≤ E[|X|^p]^{1/p}·E[|Y|^q]^{1/q}` is a special case of the general Bochner Hölder when `μ` is a probability measure.

annotations.label: Absolute moment inequality

anchor.snippet: if the s th absolute moment is finite, then the r th absolute moment is finite, too

annotations.note: `MemLp.mono_exponent` shows that for a finite measure (in particular a probability measure), `MemLp f q μ` and `p ≤ q` imply `MemLp f p μ`, i.e. the lower absolute moment is finite.

annotations.label: Hölder's inequality for product measures

anchor.snippet: Tonelli's theorem allows us to rewrite Hölder's inequality using iterated integrals

annotations.note: The general Hölder inequality applies to any measure, including product measures; combined with Tonelli/`lintegral_prod` it yields the iterated-integral form, though no dedicated combined lemma exists.

annotations.label: Hölder's inequality for vector-valued functions

anchor.snippet: we can rewrite the above product measure version of Hölder's inequality in the form

annotations.note: This abstract Hölder estimate for `‖b(f,g)‖` under a bilinear bound generalizes the vector-valued case; see also `MemLp.of_bilin` and `ContinuousLinearMap.holder`.

annotations.label: Equality condition (vector-valued)

anchor.snippet: If the two integrals on the right-hand side are finite, then equality holds if and only if

annotations.note: Mathlib has no decl characterizing equality in Hölder's inequality for vector-valued functions.

annotations.label: Equality condition in Hölder's inequality

anchor.snippet: Hölder's inequality becomes an equality if and only if there exist real numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize the equality characterization for Hölder's inequality.

annotations.label: Extremal equality for L^p norm

anchor.snippet: where max indicates that there actually is a g maximizing the right-hand side

annotations.note: `NNReal.isGreatest_Lp` proves the extremal characterization of the `ℓᵖ`-norm over a `Finset` (counting-measure case); the general integral version is not formalized.

annotations.label: Failure of extremal equality for p=∞

anchor.snippet: The equality for

annotations.note: This counterexample/remark is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Supremum not attained for p=1

anchor.snippet: the supremum is in general not attained

annotations.note: This counterexample about non-attainment for p=1 is not formalized.

annotations.label: Bounded linear functional from L^p

anchor.snippet: Hölder's inequality implies that every

annotations.note: `ContinuousLinearMap.lpPairing` constructs the bounded bilinear pairing `Lp E p →L Lp F q →L G` arising from Hölder, but Mathlib does not have a dedicated `L^q → (L^p)*` operator-norm/isometry decl in this generality.

annotations.label: Generalized Hölder's inequality (n functions)

anchor.snippet: for all measurable real or complex-valued functions

annotations.note: `lintegral_prod_norm_pow_le` formalizes Hölder for a finite family of functions with weights summing to 1.

annotations.label: Interpolation via weighted harmonic mean

anchor.snippet: denote weights with

annotations.note: The weighted-harmonic-mean L^p-norm interpolation inequality is not present as a named lemma in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Littlewood's inequality
annotations.note: Littlewood's L^p interpolation inequality is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lyapunov's inequality
annotations.note: `eLpNorm_le_eLpNorm_of_exponent_le` for `IsProbabilityMeasure` gives `‖f‖_p ≤ ‖f‖_q` when `p ≤ q`, which is Lyapunov's inequality on moments.

annotations.label: Reverse Hölder inequality (two functions)

anchor.snippet: such that g ( s ) ≠ 0 for

annotations.note: No reverse Hölder inequality is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Reverse Hölder inequality (multiple functions)

anchor.snippet: The Reverse Hölder inequality (above) can be generalized to the case of multiple functions

annotations.note: The multi-function reverse Hölder inequality is not formalized.

annotations.label: Symmetric form of Hölder's inequality

anchor.snippet: be vectors with positive entries

annotations.note: The symmetric-form Hölder inequality for positive vectors is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Symmetric Hölder for multiple vectors

anchor.snippet: The result can be extended to multiple vectors

annotations.note: Multi-vector symmetric Hölder is not formalized.

annotations.label: Conditional Hölder inequality

anchor.snippet: Then for all real- or complex-valued random variables X and Y

annotations.note: Mathlib does not contain a conditional-expectation Hölder inequality.

annotations.label: Hölder's inequality for increasing seminorms

anchor.snippet: Let N be an increasing seminorm

annotations.note: The abstract Hölder inequality for increasing seminorms on a function algebra is not formalized.

annotations.label: Hölder divergences

anchor.snippet: Hölder inequality can be used to define statistical dissimilarity measures

annotations.note: Hölder divergences (statistical dissimilarity measures) are not formalized in Mathlib.
